content:
{
  "title": "Fact-Anchor Prompting: Dynamic Evidence Interleaving for Factuality in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models frequently generate hallucinated content and struggle with factual consistency, particularly in open-ended generation tasks. Existing prompting techniques lack explicit anchoring to verified information sources during inference.",
  "motivation": "Improving LLM factuality is critical for adoption in high-stakes domains such as medicine, law, and education. Recent advances in retrieval-augmented generation show promise, but methods to tightly couple retrieved facts with generation via prompting remain underexplored.",
  "method": "We propose Fact-Anchor Prompting, a novel method where prompts are dynamically interleaved with retrieved factual snippets at key generation points. Instead of a single retrieval-augmented context block, the method structures prompts as a sequence: after each model-generated sentence or key claim, an API fetches the most relevant supporting snippet from a trusted source, which is injected as a prompt continuation. The model is instructed—via in-context exemplars—to explicitly justify statements using the injected evidence before continuing. This iterative, interleaved prompting creates a feedback loop that grounds each claim in external evidence, reducing hallucination.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on factual QA and open-domain generation benchmarks, including TruthfulQA, WikiBio, and HotpotQA. Baselines will include vanilla prompting, standard retrieval-augmented prompting, and chain-of-thought. Metrics will be human and automated factuality scores, hallucination rates, and justification relevance. Ablation studies will measure the impact of interleaving frequency and evidence quality."
}
